methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Black Semiconductor is a Series A frontier-tech company in Chips & Semiconductors, having raised €254.4M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 8 private peers and 3 public semiconductor companies (ALAB, NVDA, AMD), with direct peer benchmarking most applicable given the target's early-stage maturity and limited public financial disclosures.

The peer-median method is the primary valuation driver. A filtered cohort of 4 same-sector, same-stage peers—Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M)—yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied; the public semiconductor median stands at 20.6x across the three tickers, but Black Semiconductor's revenue is not disclosed, precluding revenue-based extrapolation.

Triangulation supports a valuation band of $1.3B to $1.7B, with a midpoint of $1.5B. This reflects the median peer result. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set (10x spread between Rain AI and Etched), the absence of revenue data to calibrate public market multiples, and the nascent and heterogeneous nature of the frontier-chip segment, where comparable financials remain sparse. The $1.5B midpoint should be treated as anchoring a range rather than a point estimate.
